watchdog_pair.next_step: Keep together in the next static and runtime-prep lane. The names exist in ntoskrnl and resolve to stable xref addresses, but the current forced-boundary decompilation is not trustworthy enough to claim semantics on its own.

executive_pair.next_step: Keep together, but hold them below the watchdog pair. They have stable xref addresses in ntoskrnl, yet the current forced-boundary decompilation is garbage-level and needs a better static follow-up before any runtime lane.

notes: The improved fallback no longer ends with 'no disassembly available'; it now records forced-boundary decompilation for every unresolved xref.
The new decompilation output is structurally useful as a proof artifact, but still not semantically trustworthy for these four addresses.
Hooke's host-side triage and the local Ghidra review both favor the watchdog pair over the Executive worker-thread pair.
